indicators.name: sink
indicators.rationale: The presence of a sink suggests a kitchen area, which is not suitable for computer work.

indicators.name: potted plant
indicators.rationale: The potted plant is not directly related to computer work and can be considered a distraction.

indicators.name: table
indicators.rationale: There is no flat work surface suitable for placing a computer.

indicators.name: cabinet
indicators.rationale: Cabinets are not suitable for computer work as they do not provide a flat surface.

indicators.name: closed door
indicators.rationale: The closed door provides some privacy, which is beneficial for focused work.

indicators.name: window
indicators.rationale: The window allows natural light, which can be a distraction and may cause glare on screens.

indicators.name: ceiling
indicators.rationale: The ceiling is too high, making it difficult to set up a comfortable workstation.

indicators.name: floor
indicators.rationale: The floor is not flat and may be slippery, which is unsafe for setting up a computer.

reasoning_summary: The indoor scene is marginally adequate for computer work (Solo). While there is a closed door providing some privacy, the presence of a sink, potted plant, table, and cabinets makes it difficult to set up a flat work surface. The high ceiling and slippery floor also contribute to the marginal adequacy.
